features.summary: Deere reported Q1 2025 results with net sales down 30% to $8.5B and equipment sales down 35% to $6.8B, resulting in net income of $869M or $3.19 per share. The decline was primarily due to planned underproduction and inventory management across segments, with some shipment timing delays pushing sales to later quarters. Production and Precision Ag sales fell 37% to $3.1B with 11% operating margin, while Small Ag and Turf declined 28% to $1.7B with 7.1% margin. Construction and Forestry dropped 38% to $2.0B with 3.3% margin due to planned earthmoving production shutdowns to reduce field inventory.
features.management_outlook: Management maintained most full-year guidance despite Q1 shortfalls, expecting to recover timing-related sales delays over the remainder of the year. For fiscal 2025, Production and Precision Ag net sales are forecast down 15-20% with 16-17% operating margins, while Small Ag and Turf is expected down around 10% with 13-14% margins. Construction and Forestry is projected down 10-15% with 11.5-12.5% margins. The company expects Q4 to show year-over-year growth as comparisons become easier, with more normal seasonality returning to most segments.
features.risks: Key risks include continued market uncertainty dampening order velocities, particularly in North America where large ag equipment demand is expected down 30%. Elevated interest rates, macro uncertainty, and high used equipment inventory levels continue pressuring demand across segments. Currency headwinds from dollar strengthening against major foreign currencies are reducing sales guidance, while competitive pricing pressure in construction markets is impacting margins. The evolving tariff situation presents additional uncertainty, though management noted limited direct exposure to recently enacted China tariffs.
features.key_drivers: Improving global agricultural fundamentals are supporting sentiment, with USDA forecasting 22% increase in US net cash farm income aided by $10B in government support payments. Technology adoption remains strong, particularly in Brazil with over 1,500 Precision Ag Essentials kits and 1,200 JDLink Boost orders. Used inventory management is showing progress with high horsepower tractor inventory declining for two consecutive months and combine inventory down over 25% in Q1. The company continues investing in R&D while managing costs effectively, with engaged acres growing 15% globally and highly engaged acres up over 30%.
